one_line: The universe is bigger, stranger, and more terrifying than you think — bite-sized cosmic facts that mess with your head, visualized with AI.
pillars: Space Horror (black holes, rogue planets, gamma-ray bursts)
Cosmic Giants (the biggest, densest, most extreme objects)
The End (heat death, the dying Sun, the far future of the universe)
Unexplained (things in space science can't fully explain)
audience: Space and science-curious viewers, sci-fi fans, the 'existential awe / cosmic horror' crowd, and anyone who likes facts that make the universe feel vast and unsettling.
tone: Ominous, awe-inducing documentary. A calm narrator describing things almost too large and too dark to comprehend.
hook_templates: This is what happens if you fall into {object}.
{object} is so {adjective} it breaks physics. Here's how.
What's really at {place}? The answer is terrifying.
{object} could end all life on Earth. Here's how.
In {scale}, {event}. This is what it looks like.
Nobody can explain {object}. And that's the scary part.
The universe is hiding something at {place}.
{object} is bigger than you can imagine. Watch.
This is the most {adjective} thing in the universe.
There's {object} out there with your name on it.

place: the center of the Milky Way
the bottom of a black hole
the heart of a dying star
scale: the last second of the universe
the first second after the Big Bang
